title: What does TTL control?
prompt_md: You write a value to the cache with `ttl=60`. What does the **TTL (time to live)** control?
options: How many times the value may be read before it is deleted
The network latency between your app and the cache server
How long the cached value is considered valid before it expires and must be refreshed from the source
The maximum size in bytes that the cached value is allowed to occupy
explanation_md: TTL is the freshness window: after `now + ttl` the cache treats the entry as expired, so the next read is a miss and the value is refetched from the source. It's your main safety valve against stale data — even with no explicit invalidation, a short TTL guarantees staleness self-corrects within that window. Choosing it is a trade-off: too long means stale data, too short means you barely cache at all.
